titulo: Prepositions of Time: In, On, At

preguntas.pregunta: [1/40] I have a very important meeting ___ 9:00 AM. Q: Choose the correct preposition for a specific time.
[2/40] My birthday is ___ October. Q: Choose the correct preposition for a month.
[3/40] We always visit our grandparents ___ Sundays. Q: Choose the correct preposition for days of the week.
[4/40] The French Revolution happened ___ the 18th century. Q: Choose the correct preposition for centuries.
[5/40] I love reading books ___ night. Q: Choose the correct preposition for the specific expression 'night'.
[6/40] The flowers look beautiful ___ spring. Q: Choose the correct preposition for seasons.
[7/40] The package arrived ___ Monday morning. Q: Choose the correct preposition for a specific day part.
[8/40] Shakespeare was born ___ 1564. Q: Choose the correct preposition for years.
[9/40] I usually drink a cup of coffee ___ the morning. Q: Choose the correct preposition for parts of the day.
[10/40] They always give each other gifts ___ Christmas Day. Q: Choose the correct preposition for specific holiday days.
[11/40] The train leaves exactly ___ noon. Q: Choose the correct preposition for specific clock points.
[12/40] Musicians made great music ___ the 1990s. Q: Choose the correct preposition for decades.
[13/40] We got stuck in traffic ___ New Year's Eve. Q: Choose the correct preposition for holiday eves.
[14/40] She started working here ___ July 15th. Q: Choose the correct preposition for specific calendar dates.
[15/40] The stars shine brightly ___ midnight. Q: Choose the correct preposition for 'midnight'.
[16/40] People usually relax ___ the weekend. Q: Choose the correct preposition for standard British/International usage.
[17/40] The concert takes place ___ Friday evening. Q: Choose the correct preposition for day-specific times.
[18/40] We always pray together ___ dinner time. Q: Choose the correct preposition for a specific mealtime period.
[19/40] The birds fly away ___ autumn. Q: Choose the correct preposition for a season of the year.
[20/40] The doctor will see you ___ 4:30. Q: Choose the correct preposition for precise clock times.
[21/40] We had a wonderful dinner ___ my graduation day. Q: Choose the correct preposition for personal special days.
[22/40] Internet speed is much faster ___ the afternoon. Q: Choose the correct preposition for fixed daytime parts.
[23/40] The store closes ___ Easter. Q: Choose the correct preposition for the general holiday period.
[24/40] Our school classes begin ___ August. Q: Choose the correct preposition for calendar months.
[25/40] It gets extremely cold here ___ winter. Q: Choose the correct preposition for seasons.
[26/40] I will meet you outside the cinema ___ Saturday night. Q: Choose the correct preposition for specific day parts.
[27/40] Humans first landed on the moon ___ 1969. Q: Choose the correct preposition for historic calendar years.
[28/40] The bell rings exactly ___ the end of class. Q: Choose the correct preposition for a specific point in time.
[29/40] My parents got married ___ May 4th, 2010. Q: Choose the correct preposition for complete dates.
[30/40] It is difficult to see well ___ sunset. Q: Choose the correct preposition for natural daily events.
[31/40] We did a lot of fun activities ___ the summer. Q: Choose the correct preposition for the summer season.
[32/40] The final exam is ___ Tuesday afternoon. Q: Choose the correct preposition for days combined with times.
[33/40] Many old castle walls were built ___ the Middle Ages. Q: Choose the correct preposition for historical periods.
[34/40] The plane lands safely ___ 7:15 PM. Q: Choose the correct preposition for precise time.
[35/40] I love watching old movies ___ the evening. Q: Choose the correct preposition for time blocks.
[36/40] We are having a big family party ___ New Year's Day. Q: Choose the correct preposition for specific holiday days.
[37/40] He usually drinks hot cocoa ___ bedtime. Q: Choose the correct preposition for specific moments.
[38/40] Jazz music became famous ___ the 1920s. Q: Choose the correct preposition for decade blocks.
[39/40] The museum is closed ___ Thursdays. Q: Choose the correct preposition for weekly days.
[40/40] The sun shines directly overhead ___ lunchtime. Q: Choose the correct preposition for mealtime moments.
